title: BUY Signal — Lower BB Bounce in Uptrend

frames.narration: XAU/USD 1H. Price pulled back to the lower Bollinger Band. The 4H EMA (orange) is trending up — we are in an uptrend.
RSI dropped below 40. Price touched the lower BB. Entry above the signal candle close. SL below the swing low. TP at 2R.
All 4 conditions met: RSI below 40, lower BB touch, 4H EMA up, London session. What is the call?

quiz.explanation: All 4 conditions confirmed: RSI < 40, lower BB touch, 4H EMA trending up, London session active. BUY is correct.

title: SELL Signal — Upper BB Touch in Downtrend

frames.narration: XAU/USD 1H. The 4H EMA (orange) is sloping DOWN — we are in a downtrend. Price rallied into the upper BB.
RSI pushed above 60. Price touched the upper BB. Entry below signal candle close. SL above the wick. TP at 2R.
RSI > 60, upper BB touch, 4H EMA down, London session. What is the call?

quiz.explanation: Downtrend confirmed by 4H EMA. RSI > 60 + upper BB touch = SELL signal. All 4 conditions met.

frames.narration: RSI dropped below 40 and price touched the lower BB — looks like a BUY. But check the 4H EMA.
The 4H EMA is trending DOWN. Buying against the trend here is a counter-trend trap. Price continued lower.
RSI low, BB touch — but 4H EMA is DOWN. Is this a BUY, SELL, or NO TRADE?

quiz.explanation: EMA condition FAILED. The 4H EMA is trending down, so buying here is counter-trend. Wait for EMA to turn up first.

frames.narration: RSI and BB conditions look met. But it's 01:00 UTC — the Asian session. London and NY are closed.
Low volume during Asian hours creates false moves. The candle that triggered RSI may be a fakeout with no follow-through.
RSI < 40, lower BB touch, EMA up — but it's 02:00 UTC. BUY, SELL, or NO TRADE?

quiz.explanation: Session condition FAILED. Only trade during London (07:00-16:00 UTC) or NY (13:00-21:00 UTC). Asian hours = no trade.

frames.narration: Gold dropped sharply. RSI is deeply oversold. It looks like a great buy opportunity.
But the EMA has turned down and price broke below the lower BB — not a bounce, a breakdown. Catching this knife means getting cut.
Sharp drop, RSI deeply oversold, price below lower BB. BUY, SELL, or NO TRADE?

quiz.explanation: Price broke BELOW the lower BB — not a bounce signal. Wait for price to stabilise and return inside the band before considering a BUY.

title: Entry Timing — Wait for Candle Close

frames.narration: A BUY signal fired. There are two ways to enter: mid-wick (aggressive) or candle close (confirmed).
Enter on the CLOSE of the signal candle above the lower BB. Not mid-wick. The close confirms the bounce is real.
A signal candle touches the lower BB. When should you enter?

questions.text: Is the 4H EMA (orange line) trending up or down?
questions.explanation: The orange EMA is sloping upward — confirming uptrend.

questions.text: Where is price relative to the Bollinger Bands?
questions.explanation: Price touched or crossed the lower BB — the first trigger condition.

questions.text: Which RSI reading would confirm a BUY signal?
questions.explanation: RSI below 40 confirms oversold momentum aligned with the lower BB touch.

questions.text: When do you enter the trade?
options: As soon as price touches the lower BB
On the close of the signal candle
questions.explanation: Wait for the candle to CLOSE above the lower BB. This confirms the bounce is real, not a wick fakeout.

questions.text: What is the correct trade direction here?
questions.explanation: All conditions met: EMA up, lower BB touch, RSI < 40, London session. BUY.

title: Stop Loss Placement — Below the Swing Low

frames.narration: You've identified a BUY setup. Now where does the stop loss go?
SL goes 5 pips below the swing low — the lowest wick of the signal candle. Not a fixed 20-pip stop.
BUY signal confirmed. Where should the stop loss be placed?

questions.text: What is the purpose of a stop loss?
options: To limit loss if the trade goes wrong
questions.explanation: The SL defines your maximum loss. If price hits it, the trade closes automatically.

questions.text: Where is the swing low on a BUY setup?
options: The highest wick of the signal candle
The lowest wick of the signal candle
The closing price of the signal candle
questions.explanation: The swing low is the lowest wick — the point at which price was rejected and bounced.

questions.text: How far below the swing low should the SL be placed?
questions.explanation: 5 pips buffer below the swing low avoids being stopped out by minor spread/noise while keeping risk tight.

questions.text: What is WRONG with using a fixed 20-pip stop on every trade?
questions.explanation: Fixed stops ignore volatility and chart structure. Some setups need 10 pips, others 30. Always use the swing low.

questions.text: On this setup (entry ~4453, swing low ~4448), where is the SL?
questions.explanation: SL = swing low (4448) minus 5 pips = 4443. Keeps risk tight to the structure.

frames.narration: Entry and SL are set. Now where does the take profit go?
TP = Entry + 2 × Risk. If Risk = 7 pips, TP is 14 pips above entry. Never target less than 1.5R.
Entry 4661, SL 4654 (7-pip risk). Where does the TP go for 2R?

questions.explanation: R = Risk = the distance between your entry and stop loss in price. 1R = you lose that amount if stopped out.

questions.text: For a 2R take profit, where is the TP?
Why must TP be at least 1.5R?
options: So that winners outweigh losers over time
Because the BB bands are 1.5R apart
questions.explanation: At 1.5R+, even a 40% win rate is profitable. Below 1R, you need >50% wins just to break even.

questions.text: What is the correct trade direction on this chart?
questions.explanation: Lower BB touch, RSI < 40, EMA up — confirmed BUY. TP at 2R above entry.

frames.narration: Nov 2025. XAU/USD pulled back hard. Lower BB touch at 4037. RSI 38. 4H EMA trending up. London session.
All 4 conditions met. Entry 4037, SL 4015, TP 4081. Price hit TP — a clean 2R winner.
Place your entry, SL, and TP on this chart. Then select BUY or SELL.
Oct 2025. Gold retested lower BB after an uptrend continuation. RSI 37. EMA up. London open.
Entry 4020, SL 4001, TP 4058. Clean 2R setup with clear swing low for SL placement.
Mark the chart: place entry, SL, TP and select direction.
Dec 2025. Pullback to lower BB inside a strong uptrend. RSI 35. EMA clearly up.
Entry 4282, SL 4265, TP 4316. Price bounced cleanly — another 2R winner.
All 4 conditions met. Mark your levels.

title: Real Example #4 — SELL Loser (Lesson: Manage Risk)

frames.narration: Jan 2026. Gold hit the upper BB with RSI 62. 4H EMA trending down. SELL signal fired.
Entry 5557, SL 5574. Price briefly moved in favour then reversed — stop was hit. A -1R loss. That is fine. The setup was valid.
Upper BB touch, RSI > 60, EMA down. Mark your levels.

title: Real Example #5 — SELL Loser (Lesson: News Spike)

frames.narration: Feb 2026. SELL signal at upper BB, RSI 63. But a major news event spiked gold higher, stopping out the trade.
Entry 5107, SL 5127. The spike hit the SL within 2 hours. Lesson: avoid trading during major news releases.
SELL conditions met. Mark your entry, SL, TP.
Jul 2025. RSI dropped below 40. Lower BB touch. Looks like a BUY.
But only 3 of 4 conditions met: the 4H EMA was still trending sideways-to-down. This is a borderline NO TRADE.
RSI < 40, lower BB touch — but check the 4H EMA. BUY, SELL, or NO TRADE?

quiz.explanation: 4H EMA condition NOT confirmed — it was flat/down, not clearly up. All 4 conditions must be met. When in doubt, sit out.

title: NO TRADE — Setup Looks Perfect But Session Wrong

frames.narration: Jun 2025. All 3 technical conditions look met: RSI low, lower BB touch, EMA up. Textbook signal.
But this signal fired at 22:00 UTC — late NY session overlap into Asian. Volume dried up. Spread widened. The move faded.
Looks perfect technically. But it's 22:00 UTC. BUY, SELL, or NO TRADE?

quiz.explanation: Session condition FAILED. 22:00 UTC is outside the London/NY window. Technical conditions alone are not enough — all 4 must pass.
